title: ochthocharis decumbens
intro: Ochthocharis decumbens, also known as the creeping ochthocharis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Africa and Asia, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The leaves and stems of the plant contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, providing health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. Ochthocharis decumbens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
